meaning: 關ｨ霑ｪ譁ｯ(蜿､蜷募ｺ穂ｺ夂視蝗ｽ)
examples: In the spring of 480 Xerxes set out from Sardis.
Sardis was the capital of the ancient kingdom of Lydia.
Yet you have a few people in Sardis who have not soiled their clothes.
That set up the Sardis Road side's clash with Llandovery, who finished eighth at the end of the regular season but had come through a play-off against Aberavon.
He has proposed building a new stadium for the Warriors at Sardis Road, and the directors of Ponty Rugby Ltd have promised to meet with him to clarify the plans.
